mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in greater than 70 percent of periods where the unemployment rate exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle signals acute supply-side stress that rapidly depresses short-term equity prices as investors price in lower output.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 235.0 trigger market expectations of aggressive central bank policy tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of periods where the consumer price index exceeds the 235.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling three-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product combined with falling unemployment creates a dual demand shock that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDP and falling unemployment yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production trading below its twelve-month moving average signals persistent capacity utilization weakness that reduces corporate profit growth and leads to negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Sixty-day forward returns remain positive when industrial production trades below its twelve-month moving average in greater than 60 percent of cases.
